terms.allograft: Tissue transplanted from one person to another — in this context, allograft bone and tissue used in orthopaedic surgery
terms.orthopaedic: Branch of medicine dealing with the musculoskeletal system — bones, joints, ligaments, tendons, muscles
terms.surgical_consumables: Single-use medical supplies consumed during surgical procedures — sutures, implants, dressings, instruments
terms.TGA: Therapeutic Goods Administration — Australian regulatory body for medical devices and pharmaceuticals
terms.ARTG: Australian Register of Therapeutic Goods — the register of approved medical devices and medicines
terms.tissue_bank: A facility that collects, processes, stores, and distributes human tissue for transplantation
terms.cold_chain: Temperature-controlled supply chain required for biological products and certain medical supplies
terms.surgical_tray: A pre-assembled set of surgical instruments and consumables prepared for a specific procedure
terms.biologics: Medical products derived from biological sources, including allograft tissue, growth factors, and cellular therapies
terms.procurement: In medical context, the sourcing and acquisition of surgical supplies, implants, and consumables
